incidence.source: IHME Global Burden of Disease (GBD) 2019, extrapolated

riskFactors: occupational repetitive stress (for knee and hip OA)
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: ACR/EULAR criteria: knee OA - knee pain plus at least 3 of 6 clinical features (e.g., crepitus, bony tenderness); hip OA - groin pain, limited internal rotation; confirmed by X-ray (Kellgren-Lawrence grade ≥2)

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Adapted ACR criteria in SUS, emphasizing clinical over imaging due to resource limits
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Stepwise: analgesics/NSAIDs, physiotherapy, intra-articular injections; total knee/hip arthroplasty for advanced cases via SUS waitlists
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Unequal access in North/Northeast regions, long surgical wait times (up to 2 years), high obesity prevalence exacerbating knee OA
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Robust SUS coverage for medications, growing telemedicine for rural diagnosis, strong rheumatology societies (SBR) supporting training
